summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Probability'?

"Probability" means: A number between 0 and 1 that represents the long-run relative frequency of an outcome occurring. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Law of Large Numbers'?

"Law of Large Numbers" means: As the number of trials increases, the sample proportion of successes converges toward the true probability. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Sample space'?

"Sample space" means: The set of all possible outcomes of a random phenomenon, denoted S. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Event'?

"Event" means: A subset of the sample space; a collection of outcomes of interest. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Complement rule'?

"Complement rule" means: P(not A) = 1 - P(A); the probability that an event does NOT occur equals one minus the probability it does occur. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

Which term is described by: "A number between 0 and 1 that represents the long-run relative frequency of an outcome occurring"

This description refers to "Probability".
